title: 8. “Something Wonderful Is Coming to an End” | Gene and Roger
format: The finale of Brian Raftery's Gene and Roger series: Ebert's post-Siskel decades, the rise of internet movie culture, and the slow cancellation of At the Movies.

segments.heading: The last act
segments.note: Ebert after Siskel, and the media meltdown that ended the show

films.title: Heat
films.note: Tied for No. 10 on Scorsese's best-of-the-90s list when he guest-hosted with Ebert, which Raftery calls cheating.

films.title: Malcolm X
films.note: The other half of Scorsese's tied No. 10.

films.title: Casablanca
films.note: The kind of old title Roeper reviewed on the audition tape he sent Sneak Previews as an 11-year-old.

films.title: Juno
films.note: One of the small, R-rated character stories that topped Ebert's aughts year-end lists.

films.title: Harry Potter and the Sorcerer's Stone
films.note: Cited as the era's expensive fantasy spectacles Ebert was glad to see taken seriously again.

films.title: The Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of the Ring
Lawrence of Arabia
films.note: Ebert's benchmark for the large-scale ambition he saw returning to Hollywood.

films.title: Star Wars
films.note: The other benchmark epic he name-checked.

films.title: Deuce Bigalow: Male Gigolo
films.note: The sort of sequel Ebert despised on sight.

films.title: Better Luck Tomorrow
films.note: Justin Lin's Sundance debut; Ebert stood on his seat to defend it against a hostile Q&A questioner.

films.title: A Prairie Home Companion
films.note: Altman's final film, and one of Ebert's last on-screen reviews before his cancer surgeries.

films.title: War of the Worlds
films.note: Spielberg's remake. Ebert's thumbs down baffled Roeper.

films.title: E.T. the Extra-Terrestrial
films.note: Roeper's example that a four-star rating is relative, not absolute.

films.title: Close Encounters of the Third Kind
The Longest Yard
films.note: The remake Ebert gave a thumbs up while panning War of the Worlds.

films.title: Home Alone 3
films.note: The Siskel-Ebert argument clip that drew half a million views decades later.

films.title: Joe Versus the Volcano
films.note: Ebert's favorite oddball; he made houseguests watch it, and only near the end did Chaz grasp how much it meant to him.

films.title: Bachelor Party
films.note: Lin's example of the show's range: reviewed right next to a Scorsese film.

films.title: Porky's II: The Next Day
films.note: Raftery's episode-four correction: he'd misnamed the film they actually reviewed.

TV: The Tonight Show with Jay Leno
